docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: Repetitionless is a

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Used to compress and extract an array of boolean values in a single integer

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static int CompressValues(params bool[] values)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description values Bools that will be compressed

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Compressed int of bools

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Compresses the input array of bools into an int

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static bool[] GetValues(int compressedValues, int valueCount)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description compressedValues Compressed int of bool values valueCount The total amount of values stored in the compressedValues

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Array of all the values stored in the input compressedValues

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Retrieves all the boolean values stored in a compressed int

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static int AddValue(int compressedValues, int index, bool value)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description compressedValues Compressed int of bool values index Index that the input value will overwrite value Value inserted into the compressedValue

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: New compressed integer with the added value

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Overwrites a value at a given index into the input compressedValues

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static bool GetValue(int compressedValues, int index)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description compressedValues Compressed int of bool values index Index of which bool value will be returned

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Value at the given index

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Gets a compressed value at a given index from the input compressedValues

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unity Editor Only

Wrapper to remove the "PropertiesGUI() is being called recursively" warning when calling EditorUtility.DisplayDialog in OnGUI within a ShaderGUI inherited class

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static bool DisplayDialog(string title, string message, string ok, string cancel)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description title Title of the window message Message displayed in the dialog ok Left button underneath the message cancel Right button underneath the message

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: If the ok button was pressed

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Displays a modal dialog removing the ShaderGUI specific warning

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static int DisplayDialogComplex(string title, string message, string ok, string cancel, string alt)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description title Title of the window message Message displayed in the dialog ok Left button underneath the message cancel Right button underneath the message alt Middle button underneath the  message

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: 0, 1, 2 for ok, cancel, alt responses respectively

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Displays a modal dialog with three buttons removing the ShaderGUI specific warning

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unity Editor Only

Base class for creating the Create/Configure Array windows in Texture Array Essentials

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description _textures The textures that are assigned in the windowShould be updated in tandem with _texturesResizing and _textureErrors _texturesResizing AUTOMATICALLY UPDATESWhich textures have been marked as resizingShould be updated in tandem with _textures and _textureErrors _textureErrors AUTOMATICALLY UPDATESWhich textures have an error that needs to be addressedShould be updated in tandem with _textures and _texturesResizing _arrayTextureFormatIndex Default: Index Of TextureFormat.DXT5The format index of the output arrayModifiable in the window _arrayMipMaps Default: trueIf the output array will transfer mipmaps from the inputted texturesModifiable in the window _arrayLinear Default: falseIf the output array will be linearRecommended in the Built-In Render Pipeline only when including normal mapsNot Recommended in URP/HDRP as it will result in brighter texturesModifiable in the window _arrayWrapMode Default: TextureWrapMode.RepeatThe Wrap Mode of the output arrayModifiable in the window _arrayFilterMode Default: FilterMode.BilinearThe Filter Mode of the output arrayModifiable in the window _arrayAnisoLevel Default: 1The Aniso Level of the output arrayModifiable in the window _resizeTextures Default: falseIf all the inputted textures will be resized to _arrayResolutionModifiable in the window _arrayResolution Default: First Assigned Texture ResolutionThe resolution that all inputted textures will be resized to if _resizeTextures is enabledDisplayed and modifiable in the window when _resizeTextures enabled _resizeFilterMode Default: FilterMode.BilinearThe filter mode used when resizing texturesDisplayed and modifiable in the window when _resizeTextures enabled or _texturesResizing has any values set to true _textureFormatSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Texture Format setting appears in the window _mipmapsSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Generate Mipmaps setting appears in the window _linearSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Linear setting appears in the window _wrapModeSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Wrap Mode setting appears in the window _filterModeSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Filter Mode setting appears in the window _anisoLevelSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Aniso Level setting appears in the window _resizeSettingEnabled Default: trueIf the Resize Textures setting appears in the window _canAddRemoveTextures Default: trueIf textures can be added or removed in the window _headerStyle Style used for headers in the GUI

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void CreateGUI()

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called when theGUI is first created

base.CreateGUI(); Must be called before performing operations

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnGUIUpdate()

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called every GUI update, used due to other tasks in OnGUI

base.OnGUI(); Will create the textures, settings, and output sections

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual float CalculateElementHeight(int index)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description index Index of the texture list which the height is being calculated for the ReorderableList GUI

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Height that the element will use

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Calculates the height of an element for the ReorderableList GUI where the textures are assigned

base.CalculateElementHeight(); Must be called before performing operations

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void DrawTextureField(Rect rect, int index, bool isActive, bool isFocused)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description rect Rect with all the available space in the elementAvailable space is calculated and can be changed in CalculateElementHeight index The index of the current element in the textures list isActive If this element is is being moved or interacted with in the GUI isFocused If this element is selected in the GUI

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Draws a texture element in the textures list

When modifying the height in any way Repaint(); should be called to update it on the same GUI call, otherwise will be updated on the next

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void DrawArraySettings()

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Draws the settings of the output array. Settings that are enabled here can be toggles with the respective settingEnabled variables

All GUI called here will be drawn within the Array Settings section

base.DrawArraySettings() will draw the settings

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void DrawFinalOutputFields()

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Draws the details of the output array

All GUI called here will be drawn within the Final Output section

base.DrawFinalOutputFields() will draw the final output details

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void DisplayWarnings()

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Displays any warnings for array creation

All GUI called here will be drawn within and at the bottom of the Final Output section

base.DrawFinalOutputFields() must be called before showing any extra warnings

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnTextureAdd(ReorderableList list)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description list The ReorderableList used for displaying the GUI

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called when the add button is clicked in the ReorderableList GUI

base.OnTextureAdd() will add a texture to the texture lists and should be called

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnTextureRemove(ReorderableList list)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description list The ReorderableList used for displaying the GUI

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called when the remove button or delete key is pressed in the ReorderableList GUI

base.OnTextureRemove() will remove the selected texture from the texture lists and should be called

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnTexturesReorder(ReorderableList list, int oldActiveElement, int newActiveElement)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description list The ReorderableList used for displaying the GUI oldActiveElement The previous index of the reordered element newActiveElement The new index of the reordered element

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called when the textures are reordered in the ReorderableList GUI

base.OnTexturesReorder() will handle reordering the texture lists and should be called

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnDragUpdate(Object[] objectReferences)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description objectReferences The items that are being dragged

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called every update while anything is dragged over the window

base.OnDragUpdate(); Must be called before performing operations

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected virtual void OnDragPerform(Object[] objectReferences)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description objectReferences The items that are being dragged

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Called when the drag is complete over the window

base.OnDragPerform(); Must be called before performing operations

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: If any textures exist in the list

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Checks if any textures exist in the list

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: protected bool CreateArray(string path, bool pingArray = true)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description path The file location that the array will be created within the project folderShould always start with "Assets/" pingArray If the texture array will be pinged after creation

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: If the operation was successful

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Creates the array using the specified textures and settings at a given path

Unity Editor Only

Many GUI functions to make inspector creation easier without MaterialProperties

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description LINE_HEIGHT The height that one line takes in the inspector LINE_SPACING The height in-between two lines in the inspector FOLDOUT_INDENT The amount of left padding for foldouts BoldHeaderLargeStyle Style used for large headers MajorToggleButtonStyle Style for major toggle buttons BoldFoldoutStyle Style used for foldout headers

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Rect GetLineRect(float heightOverride = 20)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description heightOverride Default 20 (LINE_HEIGHT + LINE_SPACING)Sets the height of the output rect

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The rect of a single line

docs.title: Description
docs.text: Gets the rect of a single line, updating the GUILayout accordingly

docs.text: public static bool DrawMajorToggleButton(MaterialProperty property, string label)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description property The material property to be used and modified for the toggle state label The text to show inside the button

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The button toggled state

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Draws a large toggle button that uses and updates the inputted property

Displays red when disabled, green when enabled

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description enabled Toggle state if the button is enabled label The text to show inside the button

docs.title: Returns

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Draws a large toggle button using an input toggle state value

Displays red when disabled, green when enabled

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unity Editor Only

Allows drawing textures stored in a Texture2DArray to the GUI as well as functions for reading and deleting the array

Automatically creates and manages its own array when textures are modified

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description Array The array used for this field TextureFormat The texture format of the array TransferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the texture to the array if possible ArrayLinear If the output array will be linearRecommended in the Built-In Render Pipeline only when including normal mapsNot Recommended in URP/HDRP as it will result in brighter textures TextureArrayGUIDrawer(MaterialProperty, MaterialProperty, int, string) Create a TextureArrayGUIDrawer using the Array and Assigned Textures propertiesThe Texture2DArray asset will be stored in a folder accompanying the material. Can be moved after creation TextureArrayGUIDrawer(Material, string, string, int, string) Create a TextureArrayGUIDrawer using a material and property namesThe Texture2DArray asset will be stored in a folder accompanying the material. Can be moved after creation

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public void UpdateArray(Texture2DArray newArray)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description newArray The new array

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Changes the array to a new one

!! Assumes no textures were added or removed !!

docs.title: Returns

docs.title: Description

Draws a texture using the assigned array

In some situations this will request user input when changing textures with a popup

docs.text: Parameter Description index Index of the texture being changed in the desired array layoutNot the index of the current array layout or textures, think of it as a constant within the set texture count sliderValue The input slider value sliderMin The minimum value that the slider will allow sliderMax The maximum value that the slider will allow content The GUIContent for the field

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Item1: Texture, Item2: Slider Value

docs.title: Description

Draws a texture using the assigned array along with a slider

In some situations this will request user input when changing textures with a popup

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Unity Editor Only

Contains various helper functions for Texture2DArrays

docs.title: Variables
docs.text: Variable Description SUPPORTED_TEXTURE_FORMATS List of all the supported texture formats for Texture2DArrays

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray CreateArray(Texture2D[] textures, TextureFormat textureFormat, bool transferMipmaps = true, bool linear = false)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description textures The textures that will be in the array textureFormat The texture format of the output array transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the textures to the array linear If the output array will be linearRecommended in the Built-In Render Pipeline only when including normal mapsNot Recommended in URP/HDRP as it will result in brighter textures

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: A Texture2DArray with the input textures and settings

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Creates a new Texture2DArray with the given textures taking into account unsupported formats

Resolution will be the resolution of the first input texture

Texture will be skipped if it is not the same resolution of the first

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray CreateArrayAutoResize(Texture2D[] textures, TextureFormat textureFormat, bool transferMipmaps = true, bool linear = false)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description textures The textures that will be in the array textureFormat The texture format of the output array transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the textures to the array linear If the output array will be linearRecommended in the Built-In Render Pipeline only when including normal mapsNot Recommended in URP/HDRP as it will result in brighter textures

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: A Texture2DArray with the input textures and settings

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Creates a new Texture2DArray with the given textures taking into account unsupported formats

Resolution will be the resolution of the first input texture

Automatically resizes all textures to the resolution of the first

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray CreateArrayUserInput(Texture2D[] textures, TextureFormat textureFormat, int[] autoResizeIndexes = null, bool transferMipmaps = true, bool linear = false)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description textures The textures that will be in the array textureFormat The texture format of the output array autoResizeIndexes Automatically resizes all textures at these indexes if required instead of showing a prompt to the userExample: If this is { 0, 2 }, the textures at index 0 and 2 will be automatically resized transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the textures to the array linear If the output array will be linearRecommended in the Built-In Render Pipeline only when including normal mapsNot Recommended in URP/HDRP as it will result in brighter textures

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: A Texture2DArray with the input textures and settings

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Creates a new Texture2DArray with the given textures taking into account unsupported formats

Resolution will be the resolution of the first input texture

Checks for user input to decide whether to resize the texture or array if a texture is at a different resolution to the array

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray UpdateTexture(Texture2DArray array, Texture2D texture, int index, bool transferMipmaps = true)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description array The array that will be updated texture The texture that will be inserted into the array index The index which the texture will overwrite transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the texture to the array if possible

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The modified array with the texture inserted

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Overwrites the texture in the input array at a given index to the input texture

Uses GPU functions to speed up operations

Update will not be applied if the texture resolution is not the same as the array

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray UpdateTextureAutoResize(Texture2DArray array, Texture2D texture, int index, bool transferMipmaps = true)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description array The array that will be updated texture The texture that will be inserted into the array index The index which the texture will overwrite transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the texture to the array if possible

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The modified array with the texture inserted

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Overwrites the texture in the input array at a given index to the input texture

Automatically resizes the input texture to the array size if it is a different resolution

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static (Texture2DArray, bool) UpdateTextureUserInput(Texture2DArray array, Texture2D texture, int index, bool transferMipmaps = true)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description array The array that will be updated texture The texture that will be inserted into the array index The index which the texture will overwrite transferMipmaps If mipmaps will be transferred from the texture to the array if possible

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: Item1: Modified Array, Item2: User Cancelled

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Overwrites the texture in the input array at a given index to the input texture

Waits for the user to input the outcome when a texture is a different resolution to the array

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2DArray ResizeArrayTextures(Texture2DArray array, int newWidth, int newHeight, FilterMode resizeFilterMode = FilterMode.Bilinear)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description array The array that will be searched newWidth The width of the output textures newHeight The height of the output textures resizeFilterMode The filter mode used when resizing the textures

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The modified array with all the textures at the new resolution

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Scales all textures in the array to the new specified resolution

Skips textures already at the new resolution

Unity Editor Only

Contains various helper functions for modifying textures

docs.text: Variable Description COMPRESSED_TEXTURE_FORMATS List of all the compressed texture formats

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2D ResizeTexture(Texture2D texture, int newWidth, int newHeight, FilterMode filterMode = FilterMode.Bilinear)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description texture The texture that will be resized newWidth The width of the output texture newHeight The height of the output texture filterMode The filter mode used when resizing the texture

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The texture resized to the input resolution

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Scales the input texture to the desired resolution

Does not modify the original texture, returns a new resized one

docs.title: Declaration
docs.text: public static Texture2D ConvertFromCompressedNormal(Texture2D texture)

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Description texture The texture that will be converted

docs.title: Returns
docs.text: The texture converted to an uncompressed normal

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Converts a unity compressed normal map back to an uncompressed one

From red to blue essentially

Does not modify the original texture, returns a new converted one
